information.overview: A card game for my friends that might get sold
information.detailed:
Before talking about this card game, it is important to discuss what led up to it's creation.
I've previously made other card and board games for my friends to play, but these suffered from overcomplicated rules which worsened the experience.
This led to me creating Slimes In The Mines with the goal of keeping the game as simple as possible.
I started out with a version printed on paper and packed into sleeves with pokémon cards.
After playing this version with my friends, it was clear that most of the mechanics were right.
But there was a glaring flaw: the point values were way too high.
After updating the cards accordingly, the game was already miraculously complete.
Fast forward a couple months, and my friends and I had played the game practically daily, so it felt right to upgrade the game.
Initially, the art was going to be vector art of human character.
I then realised that I had no art experience, and didn't feel like trying to learn the skill in a couple days.
This resulted in the design pivotting to a simpler 8-bit style which I am more comfortable with.
This new paradigm made it difficult to have expressive humans, and thus the titular slimes were born.
At this point, the game was complete, looked professional and was proven fun to play.
Hopefully I will launch a Kickstarter campaign to share the project for even more people to enjoy.

skills.explanation:
I made a game.
Lots of people said it was fun.

I designed this game first and foremost for my friends, so to ensure the game was fun I've had to make repeated revisions to the game.
This is not the first game I've made for my friends, so I looked at what went wrong with the others to design this one.
